extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won bunch sprint to equal Merckx's 34 stage record after Deceuninck-QuickStep controlled crosswind threats in final 20km
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-QuickStep operated depleted after Declercq crash yet still executed textbook Ballerini-Mørkøv leadout train through technical finale.
GILBERT attacked 66km out likely to disrupt sprint team organization after crash, forcing reset of peloton control.
CARAPAZ with KWIATKOWSKI tried right-side crosswind split inside 20km, revealing Ineos willingness to attack GC stability on sprint stages.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Fourth stage win suggests peak sprint form extends beyond flat finishes to technical, stressful crosswind stages.
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: positive: Delivered perfect leadout despite depleted team and technical finale chaos, confirms world's best pilot status.
rider_signals.DECLERCQ Tim: negative: Crash absence forced Deceuninck to improvise train composition, exposing team's reliance on single road captain.
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: Contested intermediate sprint for points but failed podium, suggesting green jersey abandonment or form deficit.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: neutral: 66km attack reveals continued aggression but failed to prevent sprint outcome or gain meaningful advantage.
